narrative: FY2024-25 is the primary benchmark year. SAIL is the clear leader on raw count, with 3 total POSH complaints and 2 upheld; Jindal is second with 5 total and 4 upheld; JSW is third with 7 total and 6 upheld; Tata is fourth with 43 total and 18 upheld. Tata’s FY2025 BRSR confirms a robust formal ecosystem: Speak Up and other reporting channels, Ethics Department investigation, escalation to the Chief Ethics Counsellor or Audit Committee Chairperson, Board/Audit Committee updates, non-retaliation safeguards, and reasonable assurance over standalone POSH indicators. The key management implication is that Tata’s issue is not absence of governance infrastructure; it is the high volume and substantiation of cases within that infrastructure. SAIL’s lower FY2025 count should be treated as the benchmark on outcome, while Tata should benchmark internally by site, workforce segment and complainant journey to distinguish incidence reduction from reporting suppression.
